title: Adding a Fomula widget to dashboards
excerpt: Currenlty you can create ‘number’ widgets and filter by certain critera which is great. However a really useful feature would be a ‘Formula’ widget so that these ‘number’ widgets can be used in calculations.

posts.author: Shannon.Cribb (Shannon Cribb)
posts.cookedHtml: Currenlty you can create ‘number’ widgets and filter by certain critera which is great. However a really useful feature would be a ‘Formula’ widget so that these ‘number’ widgets can be used in calculations.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great idea Shannon. I believe there are a few apps on the marketplace already that serve this larger function for columns, and spreadsheets, but it would be neat and clean to only display the result of a single formula like that similar to the numbers widget.

Bruce Gosk | bruce@mintconsulting.org

posts.author: JasperDemeurie (Jasper)
posts.cookedHtml:
This still isn’t possible. Which really bums me out.
I have a board with revenue numbers splitt up by month by channel.

My graph board is nearly perfect, I just cannot show the % we’re on track.
Sum of column x / sum of column y, where both values are not 0.

This is still not possible within the subscription. I cannot just just the numbers column, since the subtasks are month per month & I need the general overview.

Cumul graph is something, but this realy looks like something that should be included..

posts.author: RonnieEO (Ronnie Oliveira)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! Happy New Year to you all. I agree with Jaspe. I’m calculating the Return on Investment (%ROI) and I can even calculate it for each sub-item.

However, when I need an overview, the “Number” widget shows the average ROI, which is incorrect. There is a widget in the marketplace, but unfortunately the cost is currently prohibitive. This type of simple formula could exist in a native Monday widget.

I appreciate your attention.
